hero.title: Trusted sourcing. Japanese standards. Vietnamese craft.
hero.subtitle: Solar done properly. Built to last 25 years. SolarTNP designs, supplies, and installs grid-tied solar systems — built for Vietnamese roofs, Vietnamese sun, and Vietnamese electricity bills.
hero.imageAlt: Solar panels installed on a tile roof under a clear blue sky

about.label: SolarTNP
about.title: Vietnamese craft, Japanese standards
about.body: SolarTNP is a Vietnamese company, built around a deep partnership with Japan. We install to Japanese engineering standards, source components through trusted global partners, and rely on our own Vietnamese crew who know local roofs, local weather, and local grid realities. We are a young company — every system we've installed has been sized properly and installed without shortcuts.
about.point1: Global partners — panel quality evaluated at the manufacturing level, sourced at competitive prices without compromising standards
about.point2: Japanese standards — every mounting bolt torqued to spec, every connection tested, a 25-year mindset on day one
about.point3: Vietnamese craft — our own installation crew who know Vietnamese roofs, EVN bureaucracy, and monsoon seasons
about.point4: The combination: global sourcing leverage, Japanese engineering rigour, and local execution in one project
about.point5: International sourcing & shipping — we also source solar equipment and ship to clients across the US and Southeast Asia, through the same supplier network we use for our own installs
about.imageAlt: SolarTNP crew assessing a rooftop site against the Ho Chi Minh City skyline

process.title: How It Works
process.lead: A clear, four-stage process — from first conversation to years of after-sales support.

step1.title: Consult
step1.body: A no-pressure first conversation — your average electricity bill, property type, and roof ownership. We tell you honestly whether solar makes sense for your situation right now.
step1.imageAlt: A SolarTNP team member discussing a project with a client

step2.title: Design
step2.body: A free site assessment covers roof area, shading, structure, and your electrical distribution board. We then design your system — panel and inverter selection, cable routing, mounting method — and hand you a written proposal with one all-in price.
step2.imageAlt: SolarTNP engineers assessing a rooftop site against the city skyline

step3.title: Install
step3.body: Our own crew — never a subcontractor — handles roof mounting, cable management, inverter wiring, and commissioning over 2–4 days, using Japanese-grade mounting hardware and code-compliant wiring throughout. We also file your DOIT notification and manage EVN grid connection.
step3.imageAlt: Installers carrying a solar panel into place on a metal roof

step4.title: Support
step4.body: At handover you receive your full warranty package and monitoring app credentials. The first 12 months of callouts are free, and we monitor your system's generation so we can flag problems before you notice them.
step4.imageAlt: A SolarTNP technician inspecting a rooftop solar installation during a maintenance visit

projects.title: Our Installations
projects.lead: Four real installations. Real sizes, real locations, real outcomes — no inflated numbers.
projects.honestNote: Every install gets the same Japanese-standard treatment — full structural calculations, no shortcuts, 25-year warranties — whether it's our 4th project or our 400th. That's the standard we're building SolarTNP on.

p1.location: Private residence, Biên Hòa, Đồng Nai
p1.outcome: Monthly bill reduced from ~1,800,000 VND to ~400,000 VND
p1.note: Our first installation. Vietnamese tile roof — we designed a custom mount that preserves waterproofing integrity.
p1.imageAlt: Solar panels on a red tile roof under a clear sky, Biên Hòa residence

p2.title: Thủ Đức Townhouse

japanese.title: Why Japanese Standards Matter
japanese.body: Vietnam's solar market has grown quickly in recent years — and inconsistent quality from cut-rate suppliers is a real problem. We work with Chinese and Japanese manufacturers and global partners for their 25-year linear output guarantees and degradation as low as 0.45% per year. Cheaper, poorly-installed alternatives often degrade at 1.5–2% per year, losing half their output within 15 years. Over a 25-year system life, that gap almost always outweighs the upfront price difference.
japanese.point1: ≤0.45% annual degradation — still ≥89% of rated output at year 25
japanese.point2: Tighter manufacturing tolerances and rigorous quality control
japanese.point3: Long warranty terms, backed by established manufacturers
japanese.imageAlt: Illustration of a precision-mounted solar array under the sun, with SolarTNP's 25-year guarantee seal

warranty.title: Warranty & After-Sales
warranty.lead: A layered warranty on every installation — manufacturer guarantees backed by our own workmanship promise — plus 12 months of free support from day one.
warranty.support12mo: For the first 12 months after handover, all callouts are free. This is our baseline, not a promotion.
warranty.note: From year 2, an optional annual maintenance plan covers two panel cleaning visits and a full technical inspection. Ask us for current pricing.

row1.coverage: Linear output guarantee — ≤0.45%/yr degradation; ≥89% of rated output at year 25

row2.coverage: Parts and labour for manufacturing defects

row3.coverage: Structural integrity — no failure due to corrosion or material defect

row4.coverage: Any defect caused by installation — wiring, connections, assembly

row5.coverage: Any water ingress caused by the installation — penetrations, flashings, fixings

warranty.imageAlt: A SolarTNP technician performing after-sales maintenance on an installed system

hero.title: Pricing & Payback
hero.subtitle: Three system sizes, transparent inclusions, and an honest look at payback — sized to your roof, not oversold.

small.name: Small
small.description: Right-sized for a smaller household's daytime load — typically 4–10 panels depending on roof space and wattage.
small.price: From $1,900 USD / 47 triệu VND (3 kWp) up to $3,100 USD / 78 triệu VND (5 kWp)

medium.name: Medium
medium.description: The most common residential size in our installs so far — covers most of a family home's daytime consumption.
medium.price: From $3,600 USD / 90 triệu VND (6 kWp) up to $5,000 USD / 125 triệu VND (10 kWp)

large.name: Large
large.description: For offices, workshops, and small factories with higher daytime loads and larger roof areas.

payback.title: Estimated Payback
payback.note: Figures below are estimates based on typical system sizes and current generation assumptions. Actual savings depend on your household load, roof orientation, and EVN tariffs at the time of installation. Ask us for a calculation specific to your bill.

q1.question: Under Vietnam's current rules, how much surplus power can I sell to EVN?
q1.answer: Under Decree 135/2024/ND-CP (effective 2024), surplus electricity exported to EVN is capped at 20% of your installed system capacity. The primary purpose of your system must be self-consumption. Surplus is compensated at the preceding year's average electricity market price — not the older, higher feed-in tariffs. For most residential clients, this means the financial case for solar is built primarily on self-consumption savings, not export revenue. SolarTNP sizes your system to your actual load so you maximise self-consumption and minimise the surplus that hits the 20% cap.

q2.question: Do I need permits or approvals before installing?
q2.answer: Yes. For systems under 100 kW (all residential and most small commercial), you need to file a simplified notification with the provincial Department of Industry and Trade (DOIT) — no full investment approval required. For systems 100 kW–1,000 kW, standard permitting with local authorities applies. For systems over 1,000 kW selling surplus to the grid, a development registration certificate and Power Operation License are required. SolarTNP handles the DOIT notification and all EVN grid-connection paperwork for systems in the sub-100 kW range — typical timeline: 3–6 weeks from submission to energisation.

q3.question: Does my building need to comply with any requirements?
q3.answer: Yes. Under Decree 135/2024/ND-CP, the system must be installed on a building that fully complies with construction law, land-use rights, fire safety standards, and environmental regulations. SolarTNP conducts a compliance check during the free site assessment. We will not proceed on a building with unresolved compliance issues — this protects you as much as it protects us.

q4.question: Will the panels withstand typhoons?
q4.answer: Our mounting systems are rated for wind loads significantly above Vietnam's standard building code minimums. We use Japanese-grade aluminium railing and stainless steel fasteners, anchored into roof structure — not just surface material. Every install includes a load calculation. We've designed for the Vietnamese climate specifically.

q5.question: What roof types are compatible?
q5.answer: We work with concrete flat roofs, Vietnamese tile roofs (ngói), corrugated metal (tôn), and composite or fibre-cement roofing. Each type uses a different mounting method. We confirm compatibility during the site visit — we don't proceed if the roof isn't suitable.

q6.question: Why Japanese panels over cheaper alternatives?
q6.answer: Japanese panels (Panasonic, Sharp, Kyocera) carry 25-year linear output guarantees with ≤0.45% annual degradation. At year 25, your panels are still producing at least 89% of rated output. Cheap panels often degrade at 1.5–2% per year — half your output gone within 15 years. Over 25 years, the performance gap almost always outweighs the upfront price difference. We provide the degradation comparison data to every client so you can make the calculation yourself.

q7.question: What maintenance does a solar system need?
q7.answer: Very little. Clean panels 2–4 times per year (dust, bird deposits). Annual professional inspection: connections, mounting torque, inverter health. SolarTNP offers annual maintenance plans. For the first 12 months after installation, all callouts are free.

q8.question: Can you install outside Biên Hòa and Ho Chi Minh City?
q8.answer: Yes, within southern Vietnam. We've completed installations in Đồng Nai and Long An. We assess remote locations case by case — travel and accommodation are quoted separately for distant provinces.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This page explains what information SolarTNP collects through this website, how we use it, and your rights regarding it.

s01.heading: What we collect
s01.body: When you submit our contact form, we collect your name, email, phone number (optional), and project details (property type, location, roof area, monthly electricity bill, project notes). We use this solely to respond to your enquiry and prepare a quote. We do not collect data automatically through cookies beyond a single functional preference cookie for the language banner.

s02.heading: How we use your data
s02.body: Your contact details are used to respond to your site assessment request, prepare and send a quote, and follow up if you haven't received a response. We do not use your data for unsolicited marketing. We do not sell, rent, or share your data with third parties except where required by law.

s03.heading: Data storage
s03.body: Contact form submissions are processed via Formspree (formspree.io), a third-party form service. Formspree's privacy policy governs data processed on their platform. We retain your enquiry details for as long as necessary to fulfil your request or as required by Vietnamese law.

s04.heading: Cookies
s04.body: This website uses a single functional cookie to remember your language or privacy banner preference. No tracking cookies, advertising pixels, or analytics scripts are used. We do not use Google Analytics or any third-party tracking services.

s05.heading: Your rights
s05.body: You have the right to access, correct, or request deletion of your personal data. To exercise these rights, email thuy@tnpgr.vn. We will respond within 30 days.

s06.heading: Contact us
s06.body: For privacy questions: thuy@tnpgr.vn · +84 90 333 37 29. 529/14 Huỳnh Văn Bánh, Phú Nhuận, Hồ Chí Minh, Vietnam.

regulations.title: Vietnam Solar Regulations — What You Need to Know
regulations.subtitle: Decree 135/2024/ND-CP governs rooftop solar in Vietnam. We've summarised what it means for your home or business installation — and how SolarTNP handles compliance for you.
regulations.disclaimer: This page summarises our understanding of Decree 135/2024/ND-CP as of the date below. Solar regulations in Vietnam evolve. This is not legal advice — consult a licensed energy or construction lawyer for binding guidance.

s1.heading: The Core Rule: Self-Consumption First
s1.body: Under Decree 135/2024/ND-CP, rooftop solar systems in Vietnam must be installed primarily for on-site, self-consumption. Your system capacity must generally match your existing electricity load at the time of registration. This is a feature, not a limitation — it means the system is sized correctly for your actual needs, not oversold.

s2.heading: Selling Surplus Back to EVN (the 20% Cap)
s2.body: You are permitted to export excess electricity to Vietnam Electricity (EVN), but this is strictly capped at 20% of your installed capacity. Surplus power sold to EVN is compensated at the preceding year's average electricity market price — not the older, higher feed-in tariffs. The honest implication: the financial case for solar is now built primarily on self-consumption savings, not surplus export revenue.
s2.cap: Maximum surplus exported to EVN: 20% of installed capacity

s3.heading: Off-Grid Exception
s3.body: If your system is completely disconnected from the national grid, there are no capacity limits. This applies to remote properties or businesses choosing full energy independence with battery storage.

s4.heading: Capacity Thresholds & Licensing
s4.intro: Presented as a clean comparison table:

row1.process: Simplified notification to provincial DOIT — no investment approval needed

row3.who: Large commercial / industrial selling to grid
row3.process: Development registration certificate + Power Operation License required

s5.heading: Building Compliance Requirements
s5.body: The rooftop system must be installed on buildings that fully comply with construction law, land-use rights, fire safety standards, and environmental regulations. SolarTNP verifies building compliance during the pre-installation site assessment — we will not proceed on a building with unresolved compliance issues.

s6.heading: How SolarTNP Handles This
items: We size your system to your actual load — compliant by design
We prepare and submit your DOIT notification (under 100 kW)
We coordinate EVN grid connection approval and inspection
We document the 20% surplus cap in your system design specs
We do not install on non-compliant buildings
